text: The video displays the port placement for the robotic low anterior resection, with the camera port labeled 'C' and the assistant port labeled 'A'. Bilateral ureteral stents are visible, placed preoperatively. The robotic mobilization of the splenic flexure is shown, reaching the low sigmoid colon. Dense pelvic adhesions are observed, likely resulting from the patient's previous hysterectomy.

The surgeon uses a monopolar device for lateral and anterior dissection, revealing the distal sigmoid colon fistulized to the vagina. The dissection continues to separate the vagina from the sigmoid colon and divide the fistula. Lateral and posterior dissection proceeds down to the upper rectum, where EEA instruments are placed in the rectum and vagina to aid in retraction.
The surgeon uses a vessel sealer device to take the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and rectosigmoid mesentery. The peritoneal reflection of the proximal rectum is incised with a monopolar device.

The surgeon removes a left ovarian cyst for improved exposure, pedicolizing the left ovary and securing the ovarian artery with a clip and vessel sealer device. The cyst is placed into a specimen extraction bag. The rectum is further dissected, and the mesorectum is divided up to the bowel wall. A blue load robotic stapler is used with three firings to transect the rectum, revealing the divided colovaginal fistula.

The surgeon uses ICG angiography to identify and mark the transition between perfused and purply perfused colon with cautery. The specimen is externalized, and the diseased bowel is transected. EEA sizers assist in selecting a twenty-nine millimeter stapler, and the anvil is placed and delivered back into the abdomen. The anastomosis is performed laparoscopically using the EEA stapler, followed by removal of the EA device and placement of a bowel clamp proximal to the anastomosis. A proctoscopy is conducted, and a leak test confirms no leakage.
A 19 French Blake drain is positioned near the anastomosis site and exits through the left lateral port site, where it is secured in place.
